hyperedges.label: Director Engine bibles enforce cross-pipeline visual consistency
AI pipeline stage sequence executed by run-pipeline.ts
GitHub Actions used to work around Vercel's 60s serverless timeout for both AI pipeline and rendering

nodes.label: NOTE: This file should not be edited
nodes.norm_label: note: this file should not be edited

nodes.label: AI Pipeline on GitHub Actions decision
nodes.rationale: Vercel Hobby plan has a hard 60s serverless function timeout, but the full AI process (Research+Director+Outline+Scenes+SEO) needs ~75-120s, so the AI pipeline is dispatched to GitHub Actions runners which have no such timeout.
nodes.norm_label: ai pipeline on github actions decision

nodes.label: No ORM convention (native SQL)
nodes.rationale: Complex queries are easier to control directly via SQL, performance is predictable without query-builder magic, and there is no abstraction overhead for complex table relations.

nodes.label: Director Engine
nodes.rationale: Analogized to a film director: sets the creative vision once after Research, and all downstream AI modules must reference it to keep visual/narrative consistency across the whole pipeline.

nodes.label: Director Consistency Rules
nodes.rationale: Director must not change after storyboard is generated (unless explicit reset); every image prompt must include image_style; recurring characters/locations must reuse their prompt_anchor; no scene may use visual style outside the Director's defined image_style.

nodes.label: AI Model choice: groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile
nodes.rationale: groq/llama-3.3-70b-versatile chosen for <1s response time via 9Router; avoid creavoo-combo because it often hangs indefinitely.

nodes.label: AI Pipeline vs Vercel constraint
nodes.rationale: Never run the AI pipeline directly in Vercel serverless functions because the Hobby plan's 60s timeout is insufficient; pipeline must always run through GitHub Actions (ai_pipeline.yml).

nodes.label: tsx --tsconfig flag requirement
nodes.rationale: tsx needs the --tsconfig flag to resolve the '@/*' path alias defined in tsconfig.json; without it, imports like '@/lib/db/client' fail.

nodes.label: Consistency First principle
nodes.rationale: All assets must share the same style; Director Engine prevents inconsistencies like a scene shifting from realistic to anime style or characters changing face/proportions mid-video.

nodes.label: Scene-Based Architecture principle
nodes.rationale: Video is treated as a collection of independent scenes rather than one long file, enabling independent regeneration.

nodes.label: Everything is Regeneratable principle
nodes.rationale: Every pipeline stage can be repeated independently without redoing prior stages, e.g. regenerating one scene image without rebuilding the whole storyboard.

nodes.label: JSON Driven principle
nodes.rationale: The whole project is stored as JSON; the renderer only reads JSON, so the renderer can be swapped without changing the pipeline.

nodes.label: Render Engine Philosophy (no image-to-video)
nodes.rationale: StoryZ avoids image-to-video AI, instead composing AI-generated images with camera animation, motion effects, voice-over, subtitles, and background music. This approach is much cheaper than image-to-video AI while still producing professional-quality video.

nodes.label: No ORM decision (tech stack)
nodes.rationale: Complex queries are easier via direct SQL, performance is predictable without query-builder magic, no abstraction overhead for complex relations.

nodes.label: GitHub Actions as renderer decision
nodes.rationale: No dedicated rendering server needed; GitHub Actions runners have enough resources for Remotion+FFmpeg; rendering triggered via repository_dispatch from Next.js API; result uploads straight to storage then DB is updated; cost is $0.

nodes.label: AI module wrapper pattern (lib/ai/*)
nodes.rationale: Each AI wrapper takes a standard input and returns a typed output, so the underlying AI model can be swapped without touching calling code.

nodes.label: Bold Outlined Text Styling (Yellow/White with Black Stroke)
nodes.norm_label: bold outlined text styling (yellow/white with black stroke)

nodes.label: Desaturated Ruin vs Vibrant Prosperity Color Contrast
nodes.norm_label: desaturated ruin vs vibrant prosperity color contrast
